item.title: Vitae
item.description: INTERESTS Bayesian Inference, Structural Equation Modeling, Hypothesis Testing, Missing/Non-Normal Data, Metascience EDUCATION Utrecht University, The Netherlands Sep 2019 - CurrentM.Sc. Student in Methodology and Statistics for the Behavioural, Biomedical and Social Sciences Advisor: Prof. Dr. Eric-Jan Wagenmakers Sungkyunkwan University, South Korea Mar 2013 - Aug 2019B.A. in Psychology, Highest Honors TEACHING EXPERIENCE Workgroup Instructor Summer, 2021Department of Methodology and Statistics, Utrecht University Course: Introduction to Structural Equation Modeling using Mplus, Advanced Course on using Mplus

item.title: Tutorials
item.description: R R for beginners Heo, I., Veen, D., & Van de Schoot, R. (2020, May). Tutorial: R for beginners. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.3963825 This tutorial provides the basics of R (R Core Team, 2020) for beginners. Our detailed instruction starts from the foundations including the installation of R and RStudio, the structure of R screen, and loading the data. We introduce basic functions for data exploration and data visualization.

item.title: Reflecting upon my academic career
item.description: In retrospect, I have turned from a junior researcher who liked social psychology and was trained to find significant p-values into a methodologist who actively criticizes research misconducts and promotes the idea of Bayesian statistics. It was coincidental how I delved into the data-intensive aspect of psychology. When working as a research assistant at Utrecht University, the first role assigned to me was to code reported results for a meta-analysis. I was amazed by the idea of summarizing multiple results together.
